introduction: An inflammation of the pericardium, which may arise from viral, bacterial, fungal or protozoal infections. Other causes: metabolic, malignancy, connective tissue disease, radiation, trauma etc. May be acute or chronic.

features: Chest pain - Retrosternal - Sharp - Radiating to the left shoulder - Made worse by breathing or coughing - Relieved by the upright position

goals: Relieve distress from pain and tamponade
Treat the effect on the heart
Eradicate the organism (if cause is infection)
drug: NSAIDs - Indomethacin 50 mg orally every 8 hours Or: - Ibuprofen 400 - 800 mg orally every 12 hours
Steroids - Prednisone 30 mg orally every 8 hours and tapered
Anti-tuberculous drugs or other antimicrobial agents (if mycobacterium or other microbes are causative)
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: NSAIDs/steroids: dyspepsia and upper GI bleeding
